name: Colonel Ely's Forward Command Post
description: A captured German dugout on the reverse slope of the Cantigny ridge, reinforced with American engineering and now serving as the 28th Infantry's forward headquarters. It smells of fresh-turned earth and the previous occupants' tobacco.
atmosphericDetails: Ely's operations map is pinned to the earthen wall with bayonets; unit positions are marked in grease pencil, some of them already out of date
A runner arrives every four minutes — you can set a watch by it — delivering handwritten messages that Ely reads, folds, and pockets without expression
The sound of the German counter-barrage is clearly audible here; each impact causes a fine rain of dirt from the ceiling beams
